properties.name: Hetauda-Dhalkebar-Inaruwa 400 kV backbone
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the World Bank HDDTL RAP route description, affected municipality/VDC corridor, and route-location figure, with source-named place anchors geocoded from OSM/Nominatim where local grid anchors were unavailable.
properties.anchor_chain: Hetauda -> Harnamadi -> Dhiyal -> Nijgadh -> Chandrapur -> Karmaiya -> Lalbandi -> Khayarmara -> Bardibas -> Dhalkebar
properties.notes: Replaces the oversimplified RPGCL overview-vector selection for the public connected layer. This is a document-grounded corridor trace, not a tower-by-tower alignment. The RAP states the first 18.6 km is hill section, the next 23.4 km is hill-to-Terai, and the remaining route follows the Terai corridor. NEA FY2024/25 still lists Hetauda-Dhalkebar under construction.

properties.name: Hetauda-Dhalkebar-Inaruwa 400 kV backbone
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the World Bank HDDTL RAP route description, affected municipality/VDC corridor, and route-location figure, with source-named place anchors geocoded from OSM/Nominatim where local grid anchors were unavailable.
properties.anchor_chain: Dhalkebar -> Dhanushadham -> Mirchaiya -> Golbazar -> Lahan -> Shambhunath -> Kanchanrup -> Saptakoshi -> Koshi crossing / Tapeshwari -> Bhokraha -> Inaruwa
properties.notes: Replaces the oversimplified RPGCL overview-vector selection for the public connected layer. This is a document-grounded corridor trace, not a tower-by-tower alignment. The RAP terminates the line at the new Duhabi substation at Hanif Tole/Bhokraha; the public map labels the modern terminal as Inaruwa 400 kV substation.
